Help 导读

FAQ: first-run setup：这回它在替你排哪颗雷

FAQ: first-run setup 像排雷图。先认症状，再找该翻哪条命令或日志，最后才决定要不要动配置，不要一上来就把整面墙都拆了。原文最响的一记鼓点，其实是：Quick-start and first-run Q\&A. For everyday operations, 聪明脑袋模型s, auth, sessions, and t…。

先听这页的人话版

FAQ: first-run setup

这一页不是在堆术语，它像把“FAQ: first-run setup”这台小机器搬到桌上，当着你的面拆开给你看。你先不用全记住，先抓住它到底在忙什么：Quick-start and first-run Q\&A. For everyday operations, 聪明脑袋模型s, auth, sessions, and troublesh…。

如果把这页当成“给普通人看的版本”，你最应该带走的是：它到底在教你一件什么事、什么时候要这样做、以及哪里最容易踩坑。

原始路径：/help/faq-first-run 章节数量：3 查看原文

第 1 站

Start Here

这一节主要在解释“Start Here”到底是干什么的，以及你什么时候会遇到它。

这段在解决什么

这一节主要在解释“Start Here”到底是干什么的，以及你什么时候会遇到它。

为什么值得看

如果你是第一次接触 OpenClaw，这一节最值得看的不是术语本身，而是它背后的使用场景和限制。

真要动手时

真正动手时，先看它有没有默认值、有没有必须打开的选项、以及会不会影响安全边界。

先别急着背术语

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Quick-start and first-run Q\&A. For everyday operations, 聪明脑袋模型s, auth, sessions, and troubleshooting see the main FAQ.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Quick-start and first-run Q\&A. For everyday operations, 聪明脑袋模型s, auth, sessions, and troubleshooting see the main FAQ.

原文小纸条

Quick-start and first-run Q\&A. For everyday operations, models, auth, sessions, and troubleshooting see the main FAQ.

第 2 站

Quick start and first-run setup

这一节在讲规则和边界：什么默认允许、什么必须显式打开、什么被禁止。

这段在解决什么

这一节在讲规则和边界：什么默认允许、什么必须显式打开、什么被禁止。

为什么值得看

这种内容决定了 OpenClaw 是“能做”还是“现在还不能做”，读懂它比记术语更重要。

真要动手时

你可以把这一节当成权限说明书，真正配置时优先盯住 default、required、allow、deny 这几个词。

先别急着背术语

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="I am stuck, fastest way to get unstuck"> Use a local AI 机器人朋友 that can **see your…。

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="I am stuck, fastest way to get unstuck"> Use a local AI 机器人朋友 that can **see your…。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="I am stuck, fastest way to get unstuck"> Use a local AI agent that can **see your machine**. That is far more effective than asking in Discord, because most "I'm stuck" cases are **local config or environment issues** that remote helpers cannot inspect.

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Claude Code**: https://www.anthropic.co…、**OpenAI Codex**: https://openai.com/code…。

原文小纸条
  • **Claude Code**: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code/
  • **OpenAI Codex**: https://openai.com/codex/
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：These 工具小帮手s can read the repo, run 魔法口令s, inspect logs, and help fix your machine-level setup (PATH, services, permiss…。

原文小纸条

These tools can read the repo, run commands, inspect logs, and help fix your machine-level setup (PATH, services, permissions, auth files). Give them the **full source checkout** via the hackable (git) install:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method git ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：This installs OpenClaw **from a git checkout**, so the 机器人朋友 can read the code + docs and reason about the exact versio…。

原文小纸条

This installs OpenClaw **from a git checkout**, so the agent can read the code + docs and reason about the exact version you are running. You can always switch back to stable later by re-running the installer without --install-method git.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Tip: ask the 机器人朋友 to **plan and supervise** the fix (step-by-step), then execute only the necessary 魔法口令s. That keeps…。

原文小纸条

Tip: ask the agent to **plan and supervise** the fix (step-by-step), then execute only the necessary commands. That keeps changes small and easier to audit.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you discover a real bug or fix, please file a GitHub issue or send a PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues…。

原文小纸条

If you discover a real bug or fix, please file a GitHub issue or send a PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pulls

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Start with these 魔法口令s (share outputs when asking for help):。

原文小纸条

Start with these commands (share outputs when asking for help):

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw status openclaw models status openclaw doctor ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：What they do:。

原文小纸条

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：openclaw status: quick snapshot of 门口的小门卫…、openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s status: checks provider…、openclaw doctor: validates and repairs co…。

原文小纸条
  • openclaw status: quick snapshot of gateway/agent health + basic config.
  • openclaw models status: checks provider auth + model availability.
  • openclaw doctor: validates and repairs common config/state issues.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Other useful CLI checks: openclaw status --all, openclaw logs --follow, openclaw 门口的小门卫 status, openclaw health --verbo…。

原文小纸条

Other useful CLI checks: openclaw status --all, openclaw logs --follow, openclaw gateway status, openclaw health --verbose.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Quick debug loop: First 60 seconds if something is broken. Install docs: Install, Installer flags, Updating. </Accordio…。

原文小纸条

Quick debug loop: First 60 seconds if something is broken. Install docs: Install, Installer flags, Updating. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Heartbeat keeps skipping. What do the skip reasons mean?"> Common heartbeat skip reasons:。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Heartbeat keeps skipping. What do the skip reasons mean?"> Common heartbeat skip reasons:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：quiet-hours: outside the 设置说明书ured active…、empty-heartbeat-file: HEARTBEAT.md exists…、no-tasks-due: HEARTBEAT.md task mode is a…、alerts-disabled: all heartbeat visibility…。

原文小纸条
  • quiet-hours: outside the configured active-hours window
  • empty-heartbeat-file: HEARTBEAT.md exists but only contains blank/header-only scaffolding
  • no-tasks-due: HEARTBEAT.md task mode is active but none of the task intervals are due yet
  • alerts-disabled: all heartbeat visibility is disabled (showOk, showAlerts, and useIndicator are all off)
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：In task mode, due timestamps are only advanced after a real heartbeat run completes. Skipped runs do not mark tasks as…。

原文小纸条

In task mode, due timestamps are only advanced after a real heartbeat run completes. Skipped runs do not mark tasks as completed.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: Heartbeat, 自动小闹钟 & Tasks. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: Heartbeat, Automation & Tasks. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Recommended way to install and set up OpenClaw"> The repo recommends running from source and using on…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Recommended way to install and set up OpenClaw"> The repo recommends running from source and using onboarding:

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash openclaw onboard --install-daemon ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：The wizard can also build UI assets automatically. After onboarding, you typically run the 门口的小门卫 on port **18789**.

The wizard can also build UI assets automatically. After onboarding, you typically run the Gateway on port **18789**.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：From source (contributors/dev):。

From source (contributors/dev):

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} git clone https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git cd openclaw pnpm install pnpm build pnpm ui:build openclaw onboard ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you don't have a global install yet, run it via pnpm openclaw onboard. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

If you don't have a global install yet, run it via pnpm openclaw onboard. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How do I open the dashboard after onboarding?"> The wizard opens your browser with a clean (non-token…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I open the dashboard after onboarding?"> The wizard opens your browser with a clean (non-tokenized) dashboard URL right after onboarding and also prints the link in the summary. Keep that tab open; if it didn't launch, copy/paste the printed URL on the same machine. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How do I authenticate the dashboard on localhost vs remote?"> **Localhost (same machine):**。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I authenticate the dashboard on localhost vs remote?"> **Localhost (same machine):**

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：Open http://127.0.0.1:18789/.、If it asks for shared-secret auth, paste…、Token source: 门口的小门卫.auth.token (or OPENC…、秘密口令 source: 门口的小门卫.auth.秘密口令 (or OPENCLA…。

原文小纸条
  • Open http://127.0.0.1:18789/.
  • If it asks for shared-secret auth, paste the configured token or password into Control UI settings.
  • Token source: gateway.auth.token (or OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN).
  • Password source: gateway.auth.password (or OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD).
  • If no shared secret is configured yet, generate a token with openclaw doctor --generate-gateway-token.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：**Not on localhost:**。

原文小纸条

**Not on localhost:**

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Bad concurrent Serve auth attempts from the same 来帮忙的小伙伴 are serialized before the failed-auth limiter records them, so…。

原文小纸条

Bad concurrent Serve auth attempts from the same client are serialized before the failed-auth limiter records them, so the second bad retry can already show retry later.

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Tailscale Serve** (recommended): keep b…、**Tailnet bind**: run openclaw 门口的小门卫 --b…、**Identity-aware reverse proxy**: keep th…、**SSH tunnel**: ssh -N -L 18789:127.0.0.1…。

原文小纸条
  • **Tailscale Serve** (recommended): keep bind loopback, run openclaw gateway --tailscale serve, open https://<magicdns>/. If gateway.auth.allowTailscale is true, identity headers satisfy Control UI/WebSocket auth (no pasted shared secret, assumes trusted gateway host); HTTP APIs still require shared-secret auth unless you deliberately use private-ingress none or trusted-proxy HTTP auth.
  • **Tailnet bind**: run openclaw gateway --bind tailnet --token "<token>" (or configure password auth), open http://<tailscale-ip>:18789/, then paste the matching shared secret in dashboard settings.
  • **Identity-aware reverse proxy**: keep the Gateway behind a trusted proxy, configure gateway.auth.mode: "trusted-proxy", then open the proxy URL. Same-host loopback proxies require explicit gateway.auth.trustedProxy.allowLoopback = true.
  • **SSH tunnel**: ssh -N -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789 user@host then open http://127.0.0.1:18789/. Shared-secret auth still applies over the tunnel; paste the configured token or password if prompted.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：See Dashboard and Web surfaces for bind modes and auth details. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

See Dashboard and Web surfaces for bind modes and auth details. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Why are there two exec approval 设置说明书s for chat approvals?"> They control different layers:。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why are there two exec approval configs for chat approvals?"> They control different layers:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：approvals.exec: forwards approval prompts…、消息通道s.<消息通道>.execApprovals: makes that 消息…。

原文小纸条
  • approvals.exec: forwards approval prompts to chat destinations
  • channels.<channel>.execApprovals: makes that channel act as a native approval client for exec approvals
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：The host exec policy is still the real approval gate. Chat 设置说明书 only controls where approval prompts appear and how pe…。

原文小纸条

The host exec policy is still the real approval gate. Chat config only controls where approval prompts appear and how people can answer them.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：In most setups you do **not** need both:。

原文小纸条

In most setups you do **not** need both:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：If the chat already supports 魔法口令s and re…、If a supported native 消息通道 can infer appr…、When native approval cards/buttons are av…、Use approvals.exec only when prompts must…。

原文小纸条
  • If the chat already supports commands and replies, same-chat /approve works through the shared path.
  • If a supported native channel can infer approvers safely, OpenClaw now auto-enables DM-first native approvals when channels.<channel>.execApprovals.enabled is unset or "auto".
  • When native approval cards/buttons are available, that native UI is the primary path; the agent should only include a manual /approve command if the tool result says chat approvals are unavailable or manual approval is the only path.
  • Use approvals.exec only when prompts must also be forwarded to other chats or explicit ops rooms.
  • Use channels.<channel>.execApprovals.target: "channel" or "both" only when you explicitly want approval prompts posted back into the originating room/topic.
  • Plugin approvals are separate again: they use same-chat /approve by default, optional approvals.plugin forwarding, and only some native channels keep plugin-approval-native handling on top.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Short version: forwarding is for routing, native 来帮忙的小伙伴 设置说明书 is for richer 消息通道-specific UX. See Exec Approvals. </Ac…。

原文小纸条

Short version: forwarding is for routing, native client config is for richer channel-specific UX. See Exec Approvals. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="What runtime do I need?"> Node **>= 22** is required. pnpm is recommended. Bun is **not recommended**…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What runtime do I need?"> Node **>= 22** is required. pnpm is recommended. Bun is **not recommended** for the Gateway. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Does it run on Raspberry Pi?"> Yes. The 门口的小门卫 is lightweight - docs list **512MB-1GB RAM**, **1 core…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Does it run on Raspberry Pi?"> Yes. The Gateway is lightweight - docs list **512MB-1GB RAM**, **1 core**, and about **500MB** disk as enough for personal use, and note that a **Raspberry Pi 4 can run it**.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you want extra headroom (logs, media, other services), **2GB is recommended**, but it's not a hard minimum.

原文小纸条

If you want extra headroom (logs, media, other services), **2GB is recommended**, but it's not a hard minimum.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Tip: a small Pi/VPS can host the 门口的小门卫, and you can pair **nodes** on your laptop/phone for local screen/camera/canvas…。

原文小纸条

Tip: a small Pi/VPS can host the Gateway, and you can pair **nodes** on your laptop/phone for local screen/camera/canvas or command execution. See Nodes. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Any tips for Raspberry Pi installs?"> Short version: it works, but expect rough edges.

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Any tips for Raspberry Pi installs?"> Short version: it works, but expect rough edges.

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：Use a **64-bit** OS and keep Node >= 22.、Prefer the **hackable (git) install** so…、Start without 消息通道s/skills, then add them…、If you hit weird binary issues, it is usu…。

原文小纸条
  • Use a **64-bit** OS and keep Node >= 22.
  • Prefer the **hackable (git) install** so you can see logs and update fast.
  • Start without channels/skills, then add them one by one.
  • If you hit weird binary issues, it is usually an **ARM compatibility** problem.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: Linux, Install. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: Linux, Install. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="It is stuck on wake up my friend / onboarding will not hatch. What now?"> That screen depends on the…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="It is stuck on wake up my friend / onboarding will not hatch. What now?"> That screen depends on the Gateway being reachable and authenticated. The TUI also sends "Wake up, my friend!" automatically on first hatch. If you see that line with **no reply** and tokens stay at 0, the agent never ran.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：1. Restart the 门口的小门卫:。

原文小纸条

1. Restart the Gateway:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw gateway restart ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：2. Check status + auth:。

原文小纸条

2. Check status + auth:

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw status openclaw models status openclaw logs --follow ``

3. If it still hangs, run:

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If the 门口的小门卫 is remote, ensure the tunnel/Tailscale connection is up and that the UI is pointed at the right 门口的小门卫. S…。

原文小纸条

If the Gateway is remote, ensure the tunnel/Tailscale connection is up and that the UI is pointed at the right Gateway. See Remote access. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I migrate my setup to a new machine (Mac mini) without redoing onboarding?"> Yes. Copy the **stat…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I migrate my setup to a new machine (Mac mini) without redoing onboarding?"> Yes. Copy the **state directory** and **workspace**, then run Doctor once. This keeps your bot "exactly the same" (memory, session history, auth, and channel state) as long as you copy **both** locations:

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：1. Install OpenClaw on the new machine. 2. Copy $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (default: ~/.openclaw) from the old machine. 3. Cop…。

原文小纸条

1. Install OpenClaw on the new machine. 2. Copy $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (default: ~/.openclaw) from the old machine. 3. Copy your workspace (default: ~/.openclaw/workspace). 4. Run openclaw doctor and restart the Gateway service.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：That preserves 设置说明书, auth profiles, WhatsApp creds, sessions, and memory. If you're in remote mode, remember the 门口的小门…。

原文小纸条

That preserves config, auth profiles, WhatsApp creds, sessions, and memory. If you're in remote mode, remember the gateway host owns the session store and workspace.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：**Important:** if you only commit/push your workspace to GitHub, you're backing up **memory + bootstrap files**, but **…。

原文小纸条

**Important:** if you only commit/push your workspace to GitHub, you're backing up **memory + bootstrap files**, but **not** session history or auth. Those live under ~/.openclaw/ (for example ~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/sessions/).

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Related: Migrating, Where things live on disk, 机器人朋友 workspace, Doctor, Remote mode. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Related: Migrating, Where things live on disk, Agent workspace, Doctor, Remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Where do I see what is new in the latest version?"> Check the GitHub changelog: https://github.com/op…。

原文小纸条

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Newest entries are at the top. If the top section is marked **Unreleased**, the next dated section is the latest shippe…。

原文小纸条

Newest entries are at the top. If the top section is marked **Unreleased**, the next dated section is the latest shipped version. Entries are grouped by **Highlights**, **Changes**, and **Fixes** (plus docs/other sections when needed). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Cannot access docs.openclaw.ai (SSL error)"> Some Comcast/Xfinity connections incorrectly block docs.…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Cannot access docs.openclaw.ai (SSL error)"> Some Comcast/Xfinity connections incorrectly block docs.openclaw.ai via Xfinity Advanced Security. Disable it or allowlist docs.openclaw.ai, then retry. Please help us unblock it by reporting here: https://spa.xfinity.com/check\_url\_status.

If you still can't reach the site, the docs are mirrored on GitHub: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/main/docs </Accordion>

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Difference between stable and beta"> **Stable** and **beta** are **npm dist-tags**, not separate code…。

<Accordion title="Difference between stable and beta"> **Stable** and **beta** are **npm dist-tags**, not separate code lines:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：latest = stable、beta = early build for testing。

原文小纸条
  • latest = stable
  • beta = early build for testing
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Usually, a stable release lands on **beta** first, then an explicit promotion step moves that same version to latest. M…。

原文小纸条

Usually, a stable release lands on **beta** first, then an explicit promotion step moves that same version to latest. Maintainers can also publish straight to latest when needed. That's why beta and stable can point at the **same version** after promotion.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：See what changed: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md。

原文小纸条

See what changed: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：For install one-liners and the difference between beta and dev, see the accordion below. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

For install one-liners and the difference between beta and dev, see the accordion below. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How do I install the beta version and what is the difference between beta and dev?"> **Beta** is the…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I install the beta version and what is the difference between beta and dev?"> **Beta** is the npm dist-tag beta (may match latest after promotion). **Dev** is the moving head of main (git); when published, it uses the npm dist-tag dev.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：One-liners (macOS/Linux):。

One-liners (macOS/Linux):

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --beta ``

<Accordion title="How long does install and onboarding usually take?"> Rough guide:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Install:** 2-5 minutes、**Onboarding:** 5-15 minutes depending on…。

原文小纸条
  • **Install:** 2-5 minutes
  • **Onboarding:** 5-15 minutes depending on how many channels/models you configure

原文小纸条

How it works in the cloud: the **Gateway runs on the server**, and you access it from your laptop/phone via the Control UI (or Tailscale/SSH). Your state + workspace live on the server, so treat the host as the source of truth and back it up.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：You can pair **nodes** (Mac/iOS/Android/headless) to that cloud 门口的小门卫 to access local screen/camera/canvas or run 魔法口令…。

原文小纸条

You can pair **nodes** (Mac/iOS/Android/headless) to that cloud Gateway to access local screen/camera/canvas or run commands on your laptop while keeping the Gateway in the cloud.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Hub: Platforms. Remote access: 门口的小门卫 remote. Nodes: Nodes, Nodes CLI. </Accordion>。

Hub: Platforms. Remote access: Gateway remote. Nodes: Nodes, Nodes CLI. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I ask OpenClaw to update itself?"> Short answer: **possible, not recommended**. The update flow c…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I ask OpenClaw to update itself?"> Short answer: **possible, not recommended**. The update flow can restart the Gateway (which drops the active session), may need a clean git checkout, and can prompt for confirmation. Safer: run updates from a shell as the operator.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Use the CLI:。

原文小纸条

Use the CLI:

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw update openclaw update status openclaw update --channel stable|beta|dev openclaw update --tag <dist-tag|version> openclaw update --no-restart ``

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What does onboarding actually do?"> openclaw onboard is the recommended setup path. In **local mode** it walks you through:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**聪明脑袋模型/auth setup** (provider OAuth, 对话…、**Workspace** location + bootstrap files、**门口的小门卫 settings** (bind/port/auth/tails…、**消息通道s** (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, M…。

原文小纸条
  • **Model/auth setup** (provider OAuth, API keys, Anthropic setup-token, plus local model options such as LM Studio)
  • **Workspace** location + bootstrap files
  • **Gateway settings** (bind/port/auth/tailscale)
  • **Channels** (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Mattermost, Signal, iMessage, plus bundled channel plugins like QQ Bot)
  • **Daemon install** (LaunchAgent on macOS; systemd user unit on Linux/WSL2)
  • **Health checks** and **skills** selection
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：It also warns if your 设置说明书ured 聪明脑袋模型 is unknown or missing auth. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

It also warns if your configured model is unknown or missing auth. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Do I need a Claude or OpenAI subscription to run this?"> No. You can run OpenClaw with **对话接口 keys**…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I need a Claude or OpenAI subscription to run this?"> No. You can run OpenClaw with **API keys** (Anthropic/OpenAI/others) or with **local-only models** so your data stays on your device. Subscriptions (Claude Pro/Max or OpenAI Codex) are optional ways to authenticate those providers.

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

told us this usage is allowed again, and OpenClaw is treating claude -p usage as sanctioned for this integration unless Anthropic publishes a new policy

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Anthropic 对话接口 key**: normal Anthropic…、**Claude CLI / Claude subscription auth i…。

原文小纸条
  • **Anthropic API key**: normal Anthropic API billing
  • **Claude CLI / Claude subscription auth in OpenClaw**: Anthropic staff
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：For long-lived 门口的小门卫 hosts, Anthropic 对话接口 keys are still the more predictable setup. OpenAI Codex OAuth is explicitly…。

原文小纸条

For long-lived gateway hosts, Anthropic API keys are still the more predictable setup. OpenAI Codex OAuth is explicitly supported for external tools like OpenClaw.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：OpenClaw also supports other hosted subscription-style options including **Qwen Cloud Coding Plan**, **MiniMax Coding P…。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw also supports other hosted subscription-style options including **Qwen Cloud Coding Plan**, **MiniMax Coding Plan**, and **Z.AI / GLM Coding Plan**.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: Anthropic, OpenAI, Qwen Cloud, MiniMax, GLM 聪明脑袋模型s, Local 聪明脑袋模型s, 聪明脑袋模型s. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: Anthropic, OpenAI, Qwen Cloud, MiniMax, GLM Models, Local models, Models. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I use Claude Max subscription without an 对话接口 key?"> Yes.

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use Claude Max subscription without an API key?"> Yes.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Anthropic staff told us OpenClaw-style Claude CLI usage is allowed again, so OpenClaw treats Claude subscription auth a…。

原文小纸条

Anthropic staff told us OpenClaw-style Claude CLI usage is allowed again, so OpenClaw treats Claude subscription auth and claude -p usage as sanctioned for this integration unless Anthropic publishes a new policy. If you want the most predictable server-side setup, use an Anthropic API key instead. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Do you support Claude subscription auth (Claude Pro or Max)?"> Yes.

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do you support Claude subscription auth (Claude Pro or Max)?"> Yes.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Anthropic staff told us this usage is allowed again, so OpenClaw treats Claude CLI reuse and claude -p usage as sanctio…。

原文小纸条

Anthropic staff told us this usage is allowed again, so OpenClaw treats Claude CLI reuse and claude -p usage as sanctioned for this integration unless Anthropic publishes a new policy.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Anthropic setup-token is still available as a supported OpenClaw token path, but OpenClaw now prefers Claude CLI reuse…。

原文小纸条

Anthropic setup-token is still available as a supported OpenClaw token path, but OpenClaw now prefers Claude CLI reuse and claude -p when available. For production or multi-user workloads, Anthropic API key auth is still the safer, more predictable choice. If you want other subscription-style hosted options in OpenClaw, see OpenAI, Qwen / Model Cloud, MiniMax, and GLM Models. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<a />。

原文小纸条

<a />

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="Why am I seeing HTTP 429 rate_limit_error from Anthropic?"> That means your **Anth…。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="Why am I seeing HTTP 429 rate_limit_error from Anthropic?"> That means your **Anthropic quota/rate limit** is exhausted for the current window. If you use **Claude CLI**, wait for the window to reset or upgrade your plan. If you use an **Anthropic API key**, check the Anthropic Console for usage/billing and raise limits as needed.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If the 小纸条消息 is specifically: Extra usage is required for long context requests, the request is trying to use Anthropic…。

原文小纸条

If the message is specifically: Extra usage is required for long context requests, the request is trying to use Anthropic's 1M context beta (context1m: true). That only works when your credential is eligible for long-context billing (API key billing or the OpenClaw Claude-login path with Extra Usage enabled).

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Tip: set a **fallback 聪明脑袋模型** so OpenClaw can keep replying while a provider is rate-limited. See 聪明脑袋模型s, OAuth, and…。

原文小纸条

Tip: set a **fallback model** so OpenClaw can keep replying while a provider is rate-limited. See Models, OAuth, and /gateway/troubleshooting#anthropic-429-extra-usage-required-for-long-context. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Is AWS Bedrock supported?"> Yes. OpenClaw has a bundled **Amazon Bedrock (Converse)** provider. With…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is AWS Bedrock supported?"> Yes. OpenClaw has a bundled **Amazon Bedrock (Converse)** provider. With AWS env markers present, OpenClaw can auto-discover the streaming/text Bedrock catalog and merge it as an implicit amazon-bedrock provider; otherwise you can explicitly enable plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock.config.discovery.enabled or add a manual provider entry. See Amazon Bedrock and Model providers. If you prefer a managed key flow, an OpenAI-compatible proxy in front of Bedrock is still a valid option. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How does Codex auth work?"> OpenClaw supports **OpenAI Code (Codex)** via OAuth (ChatGPT sign-in). Us…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How does Codex auth work?"> OpenClaw supports **OpenAI Code (Codex)** via OAuth (ChatGPT sign-in). Use openai/gpt-5.5 for the common setup: ChatGPT/Codex subscription auth plus native Codex app-server execution. openai-codex/gpt-* model refs are legacy config repaired by openclaw doctor --fix. Direct OpenAI API-key access remains available for non-agent OpenAI API surfaces and for agent models through an ordered openai-codex API-key profile. See Model providers and Onboarding (CLI). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Why does OpenClaw still mention openai-codex?"> openai-codex is the provider and auth-profile id for…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why does OpenClaw still mention openai-codex?"> openai-codex is the provider and auth-profile id for ChatGPT/Codex OAuth. Older configs also used it as a model prefix:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：openai/gpt-5.5 = ChatGPT/Codex subscripti…、openai-codex/gpt-5.5 = legacy 聪明脑袋模型 rout…、openai/gpt-5.5 plus an ordered openai-cod…、openai-codex:... = auth profile id, not a…。

原文小纸条
  • openai/gpt-5.5 = ChatGPT/Codex subscription auth with native Codex runtime for agent turns
  • openai-codex/gpt-5.5 = legacy model route repaired by openclaw doctor --fix
  • openai/gpt-5.5 plus an ordered openai-codex API-key profile = API-key auth for an OpenAI agent model
  • openai-codex:... = auth profile id, not a model ref
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you want the direct OpenAI Platform billing/limit path, set OPENAI_对话接口_KEY. If you want ChatGPT/Codex subscription…。

原文小纸条

If you want the direct OpenAI Platform billing/limit path, set OPENAI_API_KEY. If you want ChatGPT/Codex subscription auth, sign in with openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex. Keep the model ref as openai/gpt-5.5; openai-codex/* model refs are legacy config that openclaw doctor --fix rewrites. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Why can Codex OAuth limits differ from ChatGPT web?"> Codex OAuth uses OpenAI-managed, plan-dependent…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why can Codex OAuth limits differ from ChatGPT web?"> Codex OAuth uses OpenAI-managed, plan-dependent quota windows. In practice, those limits can differ from the ChatGPT website/app experience, even when both are tied to the same account.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：OpenClaw can show the currently visible provider usage/quota windows in openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s status, but it does not invent…。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw can show the currently visible provider usage/quota windows in openclaw models status, but it does not invent or normalize ChatGPT-web entitlements into direct API access. If you want the direct OpenAI Platform billing/limit path, use openai/* with an API key. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Do you support OpenAI subscription auth (Codex OAuth)?"> Yes. OpenClaw fully supports **OpenAI Code (…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do you support OpenAI subscription auth (Codex OAuth)?"> Yes. OpenClaw fully supports **OpenAI Code (Codex) subscription OAuth**. OpenAI explicitly allows subscription OAuth usage in external tools/workflows like OpenClaw. Onboarding can run the OAuth flow for you.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：See OAuth, 聪明脑袋模型 providers, and Onboarding (CLI). </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

See OAuth, Model providers, and Onboarding (CLI). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How do I set up Gemini CLI OAuth?"> Gemini CLI uses a **新本领插件 auth flow**, not a 来帮忙的小伙伴 id or secret…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I set up Gemini CLI OAuth?"> Gemini CLI uses a **plugin auth flow**, not a client id or secret in openclaw.json.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Steps:。

原文小纸条

Steps:

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：1. Install Gemini CLI locally so gemini is on PATH。

原文小纸条

1. Install Gemini CLI locally so gemini is on PATH

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：2. Enable the 新本领插件: openclaw 新本领插件s enable google 3. Login: openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s auth login --provider google-gemini-cli -…。

原文小纸条

2. Enable the plugin: openclaw plugins enable google 3. Login: openclaw models auth login --provider google-gemini-cli --set-default 4. Default model after login: google-gemini-cli/gemini-3-flash-preview 5. If requests fail, set GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT or GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID on the gateway host

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：Homebrew: brew install gemini-cli、npm: npm install -g @google/gemini-cli。

原文小纸条
  • Homebrew: brew install gemini-cli
  • npm: npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：This stores OAuth tokens in auth profiles on the 门口的小门卫 host. Details: 聪明脑袋模型 providers. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

This stores OAuth tokens in auth profiles on the gateway host. Details: Model providers. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Is a local 聪明脑袋模型 OK for casual chats?"> Usually no. OpenClaw needs large context + strong safety; sm…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is a local model OK for casual chats?"> Usually no. OpenClaw needs large context + strong safety; small cards truncate and leak. If you must, run the **largest** model build you can locally (LM Studio) and see /gateway/local-models. Smaller/quantized models increase prompt-injection risk - see Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How do I keep hosted 聪明脑袋模型 traffic in a specific region?"> Pick region-pinned endpoints. OpenRouter…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I keep hosted model traffic in a specific region?"> Pick region-pinned endpoints. OpenRouter exposes US-hosted options for MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM; choose the US-hosted variant to keep data in-region. You can still list Anthropic/OpenAI alongside these by using models.mode: "merge" so fallbacks stay available while respecting the regioned provider you select. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Do I have to buy a Mac Mini to install this?"> No. OpenClaw runs on macOS or Linux (Windows via WSL2)…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I have to buy a Mac Mini to install this?"> No. OpenClaw runs on macOS or Linux (Windows via WSL2). A Mac mini is optional - some people buy one as an always-on host, but a small VPS, home server, or Raspberry Pi-class box works too.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：You only need a Mac **for macOS-only 工具小帮手s**. For i小纸条消息, use i小纸条消息 with imsg on any Mac signed into 小纸条消息s. If the 门…。

原文小纸条

You only need a Mac **for macOS-only tools**. For iMessage, use iMessage with imsg on any Mac signed into Messages. If the Gateway runs on Linux or elsewhere, set channels.imessage.cliPath to an SSH wrapper that runs imsg on that Mac. If you want other macOS-only tools, run the Gateway on a Mac or pair a macOS node.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: i小纸条消息, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: iMessage, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Do I need a Mac mini for i小纸条消息 support?"> You need **some macOS device** signed into 小纸条消息s. It does…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I need a Mac mini for iMessage support?"> You need **some macOS device** signed into Messages. It does **not** have to be a Mac mini - any Mac works. **Use iMessage** with imsg; the Gateway can run on that Mac, or it can run elsewhere with an SSH wrapper cliPath.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Common setups:。

原文小纸条

Common setups:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：Run the 门口的小门卫 on Linux/VPS, and set 消息通道…、Run everything on the Mac if you want the…。

原文小纸条
  • Run the Gateway on Linux/VPS, and set channels.imessage.cliPath to an SSH wrapper that runs imsg on a Mac signed into Messages.
  • Run everything on the Mac if you want the simplest single-machine setup.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: i小纸条消息, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: iMessage, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="If I buy a Mac mini to run OpenClaw, can I connect it to my MacBook Pro?"> Yes. The **Mac mini can ru…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="If I buy a Mac mini to run OpenClaw, can I connect it to my MacBook Pro?"> Yes. The **Mac mini can run the Gateway**, and your MacBook Pro can connect as a **node** (companion device). Nodes don't run the Gateway - they provide extra capabilities like screen/camera/canvas and system.run on that device.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Common pattern:。

原文小纸条

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：门口的小门卫 on the Mac mini (always-on).、MacBook Pro runs the macOS app or a node…、Use openclaw nodes status / openclaw node…。

原文小纸条
  • Gateway on the Mac mini (always-on).
  • MacBook Pro runs the macOS app or a node host and pairs to the Gateway.
  • Use openclaw nodes status / openclaw nodes list to see it.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: Nodes, Nodes CLI. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: Nodes, Nodes CLI. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I use Bun?"> Bun is **not recommended**. We see runtime bugs, especially with WhatsApp and Telegr…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use Bun?"> Bun is **not recommended**. We see runtime bugs, especially with WhatsApp and Telegram. Use **Node** for stable gateways.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you still want to experiment with Bun, do it on a non-production 门口的小门卫 without WhatsApp/Telegram. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

If you still want to experiment with Bun, do it on a non-production gateway without WhatsApp/Telegram. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Telegram: what goes in allowFrom?"> 消息通道s.telegram.allowFrom is **the human sender's Telegram user ID…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Telegram: what goes in allowFrom?"> channels.telegram.allowFrom is **the human sender's Telegram user ID** (numeric). It is not the bot username.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Setup asks for numeric user IDs only. If you already have legacy @username entries in 设置说明书, openclaw doctor --fix can…。

原文小纸条

Setup asks for numeric user IDs only. If you already have legacy @username entries in config, openclaw doctor --fix can try to resolve them.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Safer (no third-party bot):。

Safer (no third-party bot):

原文小纸条
  • DM your bot, then run openclaw logs --follow and read from.id.

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：DM your bot, then call https://对话接口.teleg…。

  • DM your bot, then call https://api.telegram.org/bot<bot_token>/getUpdates and read message.from.id.

Third-party (less private):

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：DM @userinfobot or @getidsbot.

  • DM @userinfobot or @getidsbot.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：See /消息通道s/telegram. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

See /channels/telegram. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can multiple people use one WhatsApp number with different OpenClaw instances?"> Yes, via **multi-机器人…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can multiple people use one WhatsApp number with different OpenClaw instances?"> Yes, via **multi-agent routing**. Bind each sender's WhatsApp **DM** (peer kind: "direct", sender E.164 like +15551234567) to a different agentId, so each person gets their own workspace and session store. Replies still come from the **same WhatsApp account**, and DM access control (channels.whatsapp.dmPolicy / channels.whatsapp.allowFrom) is global per WhatsApp account. See Multi-Agent Routing and WhatsApp. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I run a &#x22;fast chat&#x22; 机器人朋友 and an &#x22;Opus for coding&#x22; 机器人朋友?"> Yes. Use multi-机器…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run a &#x22;fast chat&#x22; agent and an &#x22;Opus for coding&#x22; agent?"> Yes. Use multi-agent routing: give each agent its own default model, then bind inbound routes (provider account or specific peers) to each agent. Example config lives in Multi-Agent Routing. See also Models and Configuration. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Does Homebrew work on Linux?"> Yes. Homebrew supports Linux (Linuxbrew). Quick setup:。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Does Homebrew work on Linux?"> Yes. Homebrew supports Linux (Linuxbrew). Quick setup:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" echo 'eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> ~/.profile eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)" brew install <formula> ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you run OpenClaw via systemd, ensure the service PATH includes /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin (or your brew prefix)…。

原文小纸条

If you run OpenClaw via systemd, ensure the service PATH includes /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin (or your brew prefix) so brew-installed tools resolve in non-login shells. Recent builds also prepend common user bin dirs on Linux systemd services (for example ~/.local/bin, ~/.npm-global/bin, ~/.local/share/pnpm, ~/.bun/bin) and honor PNPM_HOME, NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX, BUN_INSTALL, VOLTA_HOME, ASDF_DATA_DIR, NVM_DIR, and FNM_DIR when set. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Difference between the hackable git install and npm install">。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Difference between the hackable git install and npm install">

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：You run builds locally and can patch code/docs.

You run builds locally and can patch code/docs.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Updates come from npm dist-tags.

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Hackable (git) install:** full source c…、**npm install:** global CLI install, no r…。

原文小纸条
  • **Hackable (git) install:** full source checkout, editable, best for contributors.
  • **npm install:** global CLI install, no repo, best for "just run it."
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: Getting started, Updating. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: Getting started, Updating. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I switch between npm and git installs later?"> Yes. Use openclaw update --消息通道 ... when OpenClaw…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I switch between npm and git installs later?"> Yes. Use openclaw update --channel ... when OpenClaw is already installed. This **does not delete your data** - it only changes the OpenClaw code install. Your state (~/.openclaw) and workspace (~/.openclaw/workspace) stay untouched.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：From npm to git:。

原文小纸条

From npm to git:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw update --channel dev ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：From git to npm:。

原文小纸条

From git to npm:

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw update --channel stable ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Add --dry-run to preview the planned mode switch first. The updater runs Doctor follow-ups, refreshes 新本领插件 sources for…。

原文小纸条

Add --dry-run to preview the planned mode switch first. The updater runs Doctor follow-ups, refreshes plugin sources for the target channel, and restarts the gateway unless you pass --no-restart.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：The installer can force either mode too:。

原文小纸条

The installer can force either mode too:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method git curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method npm ``

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Backup tips: see Backup strategy. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Backup tips: see Backup strategy. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Should I run the 门口的小门卫 on my laptop or a VPS?"> Short answer: **if you want 24/7 reliability, use a…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Should I run the Gateway on my laptop or a VPS?"> Short answer: **if you want 24/7 reliability, use a VPS**. If you want the lowest friction and you're okay with sleep/restarts, run it locally.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：**Laptop (local 门口的小门卫)**。

原文小纸条

**Laptop (local Gateway)**

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Pros:** no 大房子服务器 cost, direct access t…、**Cons:** sleep/network drops = disconnec…。

原文小纸条
  • **Pros:** no server cost, direct access to local files, live browser window.
  • **Cons:** sleep/network drops = disconnects, OS updates/reboots interrupt, must stay awake.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：**VPS / cloud**。

原文小纸条

**VPS / cloud**

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Pros:** always-on, stable network, no l…、**Cons:** often run headless (use screens…。

原文小纸条
  • **Pros:** always-on, stable network, no laptop sleep issues, easier to keep running.
  • **Cons:** often run headless (use screenshots), remote file access only, you must SSH for updates.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：**OpenClaw-specific note:** WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Mattermost/Discord all work fine from a VPS. The only real trade-of…。

原文小纸条

**OpenClaw-specific note:** WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Mattermost/Discord all work fine from a VPS. The only real trade-off is **headless browser** vs a visible window. See Browser.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：**Recommended default:** VPS if you had 门口的小门卫 disconnects before. Local is great when you're actively using the Mac an…。

原文小纸条

**Recommended default:** VPS if you had gateway disconnects before. Local is great when you're actively using the Mac and want local file access or UI automation with a visible browser. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="How important is it to run OpenClaw on a dedicated machine?"> Not required, but **recommended for rel…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How important is it to run OpenClaw on a dedicated machine?"> Not required, but **recommended for reliability and isolation**.

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Dedicated host (VPS/Mac mini/Pi):** alw…、**Shared laptop/desktop:** totally fine f…。

原文小纸条
  • **Dedicated host (VPS/Mac mini/Pi):** always-on, fewer sleep/reboot interruptions, cleaner permissions, easier to keep running.
  • **Shared laptop/desktop:** totally fine for testing and active use, but expect pauses when the machine sleeps or updates.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you want the best of both worlds, keep the 门口的小门卫 on a dedicated host and pair your laptop as a **node** for local s…。

原文小纸条

If you want the best of both worlds, keep the Gateway on a dedicated host and pair your laptop as a **node** for local screen/camera/exec tools. See Nodes. For security guidance, read Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="What are the minimum VPS requirements and recommended OS?"> OpenClaw is lightweight. For a basic 门口的小…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What are the minimum VPS requirements and recommended OS?"> OpenClaw is lightweight. For a basic Gateway + one chat channel:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM, \~…、**Recommended:** 1-2 vCPU, 2GB RAM or mor…。

原文小纸条
  • **Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM, \~500MB disk.
  • **Recommended:** 1-2 vCPU, 2GB RAM or more for headroom (logs, media, multiple channels). Node tools and browser automation can be resource hungry.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：OS: use **Ubuntu LTS** (or any modern Debian/Ubuntu). The Linux install path is best tested there.

原文小纸条

OS: use **Ubuntu LTS** (or any modern Debian/Ubuntu). The Linux install path is best tested there.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Docs: Linux, VPS hosting. </Accordion>。

原文小纸条

Docs: Linux, VPS hosting. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：<Accordion title="Can I run OpenClaw in a VM and what are the requirements?"> Yes. Treat a VM the same as a VPS: it nee…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run OpenClaw in a VM and what are the requirements?"> Yes. Treat a VM the same as a VPS: it needs to be always on, reachable, and have enough RAM for the Gateway and any channels you enable.

像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：Baseline guidance:。

原文小纸条

Baseline guidance:

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Quick start and first-run setup”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：**Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM.、**Recommended:** 2GB RAM or more if you r…、**OS:** Ubuntu LTS or another modern Debi…。

原文小纸条
  • **Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM.
  • **Recommended:** 2GB RAM or more if you run multiple channels, browser automation, or media tools.
  • **OS:** Ubuntu LTS or another modern Debian/Ubuntu.
像讲绘本

如果把这一段摆成一个小场景，你会看到几样东西正在互相打招呼、拦路或者传东西。别急着记名词，先抓住它此刻到底在发生什么：If you are on Windows, **WSL2 is the easiest VM style setup** and has the best 工具小帮手ing compatibility. See Windows, VPS…。

原文小纸条

If you are on Windows, **WSL2 is the easiest VM style setup** and has the best tooling compatibility. See Windows, VPS hosting. If you are running macOS in a VM, see macOS VM. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 3 站

Related

这一节更像安装或配置步骤，重点不是概念，而是“按什么顺序做才不会卡住”。

这段在解决什么

这一节更像安装或配置步骤，重点不是概念，而是“按什么顺序做才不会卡住”。

为什么值得看

很多文档看起来长，其实是在防你漏掉前置条件。

真要动手时

真正照做时，先找前置条件，再找必填项，最后看验证方法。

先别急着背术语

这一小段像旁白，在提醒我们镜头已经切到下一站。

像准备清单

这一串条目别硬背，把它当成“Related”门口贴出来的几张便签就行。它们在提醒你先备好什么、别漏掉什么、哪里最容易走错：FAQ — the main FAQ (聪明脑袋模型s, sessions, 门口…、Install overview、Getting started、Troubleshooting。
